channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Highguard Player Count Has Almost Hit Double-Digit Territory
item.content:encoded:
Highguard continues to flounder following the game’s disastrous launch, and nothing we’re hearing from both the project and the studio at Wildlight Entertainment bodes well. In January, Highguard dropped after almost two months of radio silence from the developer and promptly fell flat on its face, marking one of the biggest failures in gaming history.

Earlier today, the game’s player count (verifiable only on Steam) dropped dangerously close to double-digit territory, falling to just 105 active users on Valve’s PC platform.

Highguard Has Fallen

There’s no saving Highguard now, but that has been apparent for a while.

The game launched at the end of January to an utterly miserable reception, being eagerly cast out by critics worldwide. Within weeks, the player count had dwindled, Wildlight Entertainment had held layoffs, and the rumors started leaking from within the project, none of them saying anything positive about the ‘new breed of shooter’ title.

Even the game’s former developers had their concerns about the game long before it hit the market.

Looking at Highguard’s numbers, we can paint a fairly awful picture. This morning, it dropped to just 105 players on Steam, the only platform on which player numbers can be accurately verified. That’s a mighty -99.8% drop from the all-time peak of 97,249, secured when the game came out.

It’s not just players, though. Those interested in watching Highguard content gave up on the game long ago, and at the time of writing, just 46 people in the entire world were tuned into a Highguard stream on Twitch. On January 26, 382,000 viewers were watching, making up their mind as to whether they wanted to give the game the time of day or not.

It could have been worse, too. Highguard was supposed to be shadowdropped, but Geoff Keighley was reportedly adamant to have it occupy the prestigious final reveal spot at The Game Awards at the tail end of 2025.

It’s never nice to see a game die, especially not one that was revealed with such gusto and aura. It became the talk of the town for a while, but even a creator-backed investment couldn’t do anything to muster up traction at launch. The path forward for Highguard is effectively non-existent at this point, and that’s a shame.

Did you play Highguard? Let us know what you thought about it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the ‘next Palworld’ is soaring on Kickstarter

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Has The Final Puzzle Been Solved In Resident Evil Requiem? Live Tracker
item.content:encoded:
When you think you’ve done everything in Resident Evil Requiem, there’s likely to be one challenge left if you don’t know how to complete it: The Final Puzzle. Questions about how to solve it have battered the game’s early days.

‘The Final Puzzle’ is one of the many challenges in Resident Evil Requiem. Don’t be surprised if it’s the last one you end up ticking off, if ever. It’s extremely mysterious, and even Capcom has given very little away about it.

Has The Final Puzzle Been Completed Yet In Resident Evil Requiem?

Officially, it’s unclear if The Final Puzzle has been completed in Resident Evil Requiem, as one YouTube content creator claims to have done it, but not everyone is convinced.

As of March 2, 2026, it seems like only one person has claimed to have beaten The Final Puzzle: Gengar Collects.

In their ‘Possible World’s First To Solve The Final Puzzle In Resident Evil Requiem!’ video, they showed their challenge list, with The Final Puzzle marked as completed with a check mark.

  • More Insider Gaming: Roulette Easter Egg Puzzle Solution In Resident Evil Requiem

There has been much confusion about which items you need and how to get them for The Final Puzzle, particularly Marie’s Doll. Gengar claims to have picked up Marie’s Doll during Grace’s escape in the underground lab after Victor Gideon triggers the self-destruct sequence.

If you check the Resident Evil main Reddit, X, or anywhere else, no one seems to be able to get the doll to spawn—making this either the biggest slice of fortune imaginable, or the claim isn’t true—Insider Gaming does not know, but no one else seems to be able to replicate this.

We’ll mark The Final Puzzle as unsolved for now, until an established solution is found that everyone can trigger.

Stay tuned for more updates on The Final Puzzle mystery.

What Is The Final Puzzle In Resident Evil 9? All Clues & Answers So Far

The Final Puzzle is a convoluted challenge that requires you to input a very specific and long strand of code, while having certain items in your inventory, such as the Severed Hand and Marie’s Doll.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Open Examination Room Safe In Resident Evil Requiem

Here’s everything known about The Final Puzzle to this point (with a lot of help from The Final Puzzle subreddit):

  • You need to pick up the Severed Hand and analyze it using one of Requiem’s two Laser Microscopes.
  • It gives you this message: Let’s play. GGC AAG AUA ACG UGU CAU.
  • When you find the Hourglass in the hunt for the Helicopter key, tip it upside down to remove all sand, and it reveals a message: U = 380,000.
  • The Moon is roughly 380,000 kilometers from the Earth, making U represent Moon.
  • The Research Facility beneath the Care Center’s Courtyard has Grace Ashcroft’s Blood Analysis Report.
  • C = 4.2 ly is the final entry, and according to the subreddit (because this is too smart for us): “The distance to the nearest Star (that is not the Sun) happens to be 4.2 light years away.”
  • Making C represent Star.
  • Furthermore, on the side of the Laser Microscope in the Blood Lab, it says ‘G = 150,000,000.’
  • Long story short, ignore the science; it’s the sun. G represents the Sun.
  • The mystery surrounding the letter ‘A’ borderlines Sherlock Holmes-levels of detective work.
    • Defeat Super Tyrant behind RPD.
    • Access the shortcut through the basketball court.
    • Kick the Toy Uncle Bobblehead on the ground into the fallen hoop nearby.
    • You should hear a door unlocking sound effect.
    • Go to the bobblehead in Kendo’s Shop, interact with the torso, and it says: “Just ignore A.“
  • All values are now known for ‘GGC AAG AUA ACG UGU CAU.’
  • Enter the following into the puzzle machine in the Lead Researcher’s Office: Sun, Sun, Star, then Sun, Moon, Star, then Sun, Moon, Sun, and finally Moon, Star, Moon.
  • A child’s laughter plays, but this still isn’t enough to solve The Final Puzzle right now!

Check back for more updates, new clues solved, and if The Final Puzzle can finally be completed.

Until then, feel free to discuss it with the IG community, and if you have clues or ideas yourself anymore, tell the Insider Gaming Discord; you never know, YOU could be the one to solve it.

Check out more handy RE9 guides:

  • How To Get All ID Wristbands In Resident Evil Requiem
  • Complete Organless Corpse Walkthrough In Resident Evil Requiem
  • How To Open Bar & Lounge Safe In Resident Evil Requiem
  • How To Get Pantry Key In Resident Evil Requiem

item.title: Pokopia Sets Record As Highest-Rated Pokémon Game Of All Time
item.content:encoded:
Pokopia has set the record as the highest-rated game ever in the Pokémon franchise, with the Nintendo Switch 2 exclusive making waves ahead of this week’s release.

Pokémon Pokopia releases March 5 on Nintendo Switch 2, marking the franchise’s first entry into the life-simulation genre in Pokémon‘s 30-year history, and critics have largely been blown away.

The lifting of the review embargo on Pokopia on March 2, three days before release, led to a flood of praise for the title, making it the highest-rated score on Metacritic of all time for the Pokémon franchise.

Pokopia boasts a Metacritic score of 89, the highest total ever, narrowly beating 2013’s Pokémon X and the 2010 classic Pokémon Heart Gold.

It also marks the first time since a Pokémon title on Nintendo Switch platforms has earned a Metacritic score above 85, with the most recent entries in the franchise boasting a higher score coming from the 87-rated Pokémon Sun and Moon on Nintendo 3DS a decade ago.

2022’s Pokémon Legends: Arceus was previously the highest-rated Pokémon title on Nintendo Switch, with a Metacritic rating of 83, and a score of 89 for Pokopia blows away a slew of recent releases for the franchise.

The Generation 9 games, Pokémon Scarlet and Violet, were littered with performance problems upon release, which were reflected in the critic scores, while Pokémon Legends: Z-A, the most recent Pokémon title before the release of Pokopia, scored 78.

Pokopia’s scores will certainly raise excitement ahead of the full release of the game this week, and, although not guaranteed, the expectation is that user scores will be similarly high, with demo experiences of Pokopia at the likes of EUIC making a swift impression on fans.

Will Pokopia be the greatest game ever in Pokémon history? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Pokopia, check out exactly what time the game releases in your region, and find out all the platform details for the game.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NACON Delays Connect Showcase to Prioritize Games
item.content:encoded:
NACON provides an update on its Connect event and confirms financial difficulties.

NACON recently announced that it was in danger of closing after filing for an emergency insolvency. The company has now released a new statement confirming that it will postpone its annual NACON Connect event. The showcase was originally scheduled for March 4, but it has been postponed to focus even harder on its upcoming games. It will now be held in May 2026.

NACON Says This Isn’t the End for Them

In the statement, they also confirm that they are in a challenging financial position, but the company is not at its end. They say they are confident in their ability to get through this hardship and look forward to bringing the Connect event back as soon as possible. I also confirmed that their announced releases remain on track and they have additional projects in the works.

Read their full statement below:

“Hi everyone,

You may have seen reports about NACON’s financial situation.

It’s true: this is a difficult moment for us, and we want to address it directly.

Yes, things are challenging. No, this is not the end.

We’ve decided to postpone NACON Connect to work even harder on the launch of upcoming games and unveil the new games once our situation will be stabilized.

What hasn’t changed: our announced releases remain on track, and we still have many projects in development.

We’re confident we’ll get through this, and we look forward to bringing NACON Connect back as soon as we can — to focus on what matters most: our games and accessories.

Thank you for your continued support.

The NACON Team”

One of the games that remains on track for the studio is Terminator: Survivors. Learn the latest on that game’s development here.

Insider Gaming will continue to bring you the latest updates on this situation as more are made available.

How do you feel about the situation NACON is facing? Are you looking forward to the upcoming Terminator game?

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nacon-delays-connect-showcase-to-prioritize-games/

item.title: Nintendo Announces Indie World Showcase For This Week
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has officially confirmed that its next Indie World Showcase will air on March 3, 2026, offering fans another glimpse at the exciting slate of independent titles heading to the Nintendo Switch in the months ahead.

The announcement was made via Nintendo Today!, with the presentation set to begin at 6 AM PT. As with previous Indie World broadcasts, viewers can expect a focused showcase highlighting a curated selection of indie projects, ranging from brand-new reveals to release date announcements.

Latest Indie World Showcase Is Imminent

Details remain relatively scarce for the upcoming Indie World Showcase, but Nintendo has revealed that it will be roughly 15 minutes long, so you shouldn’t need to set aside too much time if you want to catch it live.

The previous Indie World Showcase took place back in August, 2025, and it featured a showcase of similar length, full of new trailers and showcases. Some of the highlights were Balatro, Pizza Tower and Neva; fingers crossed that the upcoming 2026 showcase has just as much on offer.

With both the Nintendo Switch and Nintendo Switch 2 continuing to serve as major platforms for indie developers, anticipation is building over what surprises could be in store. Whether you’re hoping for shadow drops, release dates, or entirely new IP reveals, this showcase could have something worth keeping an eye on.

If you’re curious about what will be revealed but won’t have the time to catch the show live, then be sure to check back at Insider Gaming for a complete roundup of everything announced at the March 3 Indie World Showcase.

What are you hoping to see at the Indie World Showcase? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming news, check out the Starfield PS5 release date, as well as the breaking news that a new Gran Turismo is in active development.
